title: GameStop Q2 2025 Earnings Summary
text: GameStop reported strong second quarter 2025 results with net sales of $972.2 million, a 22% increase from $798.3 million in the prior year period. The company swung to profitability with net income of $168.6 million compared to $14.8 million a year earlier. Operating income was $66.4 million versus an operating loss of $22.0 million. SG&A expenses declined significantly to $218.8 million from $270.8 million year-over-year. Cash position reached $8.7 billion in cash and equivalents, nearly double the prior year's $4.2 billion. Bitcoin holdings totaled $528.6 million in digital assets. For the 26-week period ended August 2, 2025: Net sales reached $1.7 billion. Net income was $213.4 million versus a $17.5 million loss in the prior year. Operating income improved to $55.6 million from a $72.6 million loss. The company divested Canadian operations during Q2 and is pursuing divestiture of its France location. Management emphasized transformation initiatives focusing on cost reduction and operational efficiency while maintaining strong cash generation.
